---
title: NYISO Gold Book — Peak Demand & Building Electrification
reference-location: margin
fig-cap-location: bottom
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This notebook loads the 2025 NYISO Gold Book forecast data from S3 and produces
figures used in the ny_hp_rates report:

1. **NYCA building electrification incremental load growth** — baseline and lower
   demand scenarios, 2025–2055 (Table I-13c / I-13c-L).
2. **NYCA households converted to electric heating** — cumulative band edges digitized
   from the NYISO chart image
   `img/nyiso_nyca_residential_electric_heating_by_technology_reference.jpg`; each
   layer’s thickness (housing units) at those knots is fit with a **logistic (sigmoid)**
   in % of NY residential electric customers (EIA-861 on S3). Points = digitized
   readings; ribbons = fitted S-curves.
3. **NYCA coincident peak demand forecast** — summer and winter peaks with a
   highlighted ribbon showing the building electrification contribution and a
   dashed "without electrification" counterfactual line (Tables I-3a, I-3b,
   I-13b, I-13c).
4. **Per-utility demand component breakdown** — stacked area chart of all winter
   coincident peak demand components (building electrification, EV, large loads,
   EE, non-solar DG, storage) as % of the 2023-24 coincident winter peak, with
   total CP growth overlaid and headroom exhaustion years marked (NYISO Gold Book
   I-series tables; Synapse July 2024 headroom assessment).

The headroom table from the Synapse Energy Economics distribution headroom
analysis (Takahashi et al., July 2024) is read from S3 as a reference for
the per-utility headroom thresholds.

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup

from __future__ import annotations

import pickle
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import openpyxl
import polars as pl
from lib.plotnine import SB_COLORS, theme_switchbox
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    annotate,
    coord_cartesian,
    element_blank,
    element_text,
    facet_wrap,
    geom_hline,
    geom_line,
    geom_point,
    geom_ribbon,
    geom_text,
    geom_vline,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    scale_color_manual,
    scale_fill_manual,
    scale_linetype_manual,
    scale_x_continuous,
    scale_y_continuous,
    theme,
)
from scipy.optimize import curve_fit

report_vars: dict = {}

CACHE_DIR = Path("../cache")
CACHE_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
```

## Parameters

```{python}
#| label: params

S3_BASELINE = "s3://data.sb/nyiso/gold_book/2025_baseline_forecast_tables.xlsx"
S3_LOWER = "s3://data.sb/nyiso/gold_book/2025_lower_demand_forecast_tables.xlsx"
S3_HEADROOM = "s3://data.sb/nyiso/gold_book/synapse_headroom_table.csv"

PATH_BASELINE = CACHE_DIR / "gb_2025_baseline.xlsx"
PATH_LOWER = CACHE_DIR / "gb_2025_lower.xlsx"
PATH_HEADROOM = CACHE_DIR / "synapse_headroom_table.csv"

# NYISO load zones A-K
ZONES = ["A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G", "H", "I", "J", "K"]

# Zone assignments — same lists as rate-design-platform
# `compute_headroom_exhaustion_year.py` (UTILITY_ZONES). National Grid and
# NYSEG & RGE both sum zones A–F; Con Edison includes G+H+I+J; CH and O&R each
# use zone G only (zone G is triple-counted across those three — matches RDP).
UTILITY_ZONES: dict[str, list[str]] = {
    "National Grid":     ["A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F"],
    "NYSEG & RGE":       ["A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F"],
    "Central Hudson":    ["G"],
    "Con Edison":        ["G", "H", "I", "J"],
    "Orange & Rockland": ["G"],
}

# Synapse headroom as % of each utility's baseline winter NCP (Takahashi et al. 2024).
HEADROOM_PCT: dict[str, float] = {
    "National Grid":     1.05,
    "NYSEG & RGE":       0.85,
    "Central Hudson":    0.35,
    "Con Edison":        0.29,
    "Orange & Rockland": 0.95,
}

# Headroom: absolute I-4b NCP ≥ (1 + HEADROOM_PCT) × zone-sum NCP at this season.
HEADROOM_BASELINE_YEAR = "2023-24"

# Normalization year for stacked ribbons and CP growth line (% of that year’s CP, I-3b).
# Matches rate-design-platform `plot_demand_components_by_utility(..., norm_year="2023-24")`.
NORM_YEAR = "2023-24"

# Forecast horizon (winter season starting year)
FORECAST_START = 2025
FORECAST_END = 2055
```

## Download Gold Book files from S3

We cache the Excel files locally to avoid re-downloading on every render.

```{python}
#| label: download-s3

def _s3_cp(src: str, dst: Path) -> None:
    """Download a file from S3 to a local path if not already cached."""
    if dst.exists():
        return
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["aws", "s3", "cp", src, str(dst)],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        raise RuntimeError(f"aws s3 cp failed: {result.stderr}")

_s3_cp(S3_BASELINE, PATH_BASELINE)
_s3_cp(S3_LOWER, PATH_LOWER)
_s3_cp(S3_HEADROOM, PATH_HEADROOM)

print(f"Baseline:  {PATH_BASELINE} ({PATH_BASELINE.stat().st_size:,} bytes)")
print(f"Lower:     {PATH_LOWER} ({PATH_LOWER.stat().st_size:,} bytes)")
print(f"Headroom:  {PATH_HEADROOM} ({PATH_HEADROOM.stat().st_size:,} bytes)")
```

## Parse Gold Book data

The Gold Book uses two year-column formats depending on the table:

- **Winter season strings** like `"2024-25"` — used by peak demand tables (I-3b,
  I-4b, I-13c, I-11d, I-8c).
- **Calendar year integers** like `2024` — used by energy/storage tables (I-3a,
  I-13a, I-13b, I-10c, I-12c). These are normalised to `"YYYY-YY"` keys.

Both parsers return `{year_str: {zone_letter: MW}}` dicts.

```{python}
#| label: parsers

def _parse_winter_str(path: Path, sheet: str) -> dict[str, dict[str, int]]:
    """Parse a Gold Book table with 'YYYY-YY' year column (e.g. I-3b, I-13c)."""
    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(path, read_only=True, data_only=True)
    ws = wb[sheet]
    data: dict[str, dict[str, int]] = {}
    for row in ws.iter_rows(min_row=7, values_only=True):
        year = row[2]
        if not isinstance(year, str) or "-" not in year:
            continue
        vals = row[3:14]
        if any(v is None for v in vals):
            continue
        data[year] = {z: int(v) for z, v in zip(ZONES, vals)}
    wb.close()
    return data


def _parse_calendar_int(path: Path, sheet: str) -> dict[str, dict[str, int]]:
    """Parse a Gold Book table with integer calendar year (e.g. I-3a, I-13b).

    Keys are normalised to 'YYYY-YY' winter season strings so all tables share
    the same key format (calendar year N maps to winter 'N-(N+1)').
    """
    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(path, read_only=True, data_only=True)
    ws = wb[sheet]
    data: dict[str, dict[str, int]] = {}
    for row in ws.iter_rows(min_row=7, values_only=True):
        year = row[2]
        if not isinstance(year, int):
            continue
        vals = row[3:14]
        if any(v is None for v in vals):
            continue
        key = f"{year}-{(year + 1) % 100:02d}"
        data[key] = {z: int(v) for z, v in zip(ZONES, vals)}
    wb.close()
    return data


def _parse_large_loads(path: Path, sheet: str = "I-14") -> dict[str, dict[str, int]]:
    """Parse Table I-14 / I-14-L (Large Loads winter peak, rows start at row 41).

    The Gold Book only forecasts through ~2035-36; values are forward-filled
    through 2056-57 per the Gold Book's own note.
    """
    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(path, read_only=True, data_only=True)
    ws = wb[sheet]
    data: dict[str, dict[str, int]] = {}
    for row in ws.iter_rows(min_row=41, values_only=True):
        year = row[2]
        if not isinstance(year, str) or "-" not in year:
            continue
        vals = row[3:14]
        if any(v is None for v in vals):
            continue
        data[year] = {z: int(v) for z, v in zip(ZONES, vals)}
    wb.close()
    # Forward-fill from the last available row through the forecast horizon
    if data:
        last_year = max(data.keys())
        last_vals = data[last_year]
        for yr in range(int(last_year.split("-")[0]) + 1, FORECAST_END + 2):
            key = f"{yr}-{(yr + 1) % 100:02d}"
            data[key] = last_vals
    return data


def _parse_large_loads_summer(path: Path, sheet: str = "I-14") -> dict[str, dict[str, int]]:
    """Parse I-14 Summer Peak Demand section (rows 29–44, calendar year ints).

    Same forward-fill logic as `_parse_large_loads` for winter.
    """
    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(path, read_only=True, data_only=True)
    ws = wb[sheet]
    data: dict[str, dict[str, int]] = {}
    for row in ws.iter_rows(min_row=29, max_row=44, values_only=True):
        year = row[2]
        if not isinstance(year, int):
            continue
        vals = row[3:14]
        if any(v is None for v in vals):
            continue
        key = f"{year}-{(year + 1) % 100:02d}"
        data[key] = {z: int(v) for z, v in zip(ZONES, vals)}
    wb.close()
    if data:
        last_year = max(data.keys())
        last_vals = data[last_year]
        for yr in range(int(last_year.split("-")[0]) + 1, FORECAST_END + 2):
            key = f"{yr}-{(yr + 1) % 100:02d}"
            data[key] = last_vals
    return data


def _nyca(zone_data: dict[str, dict[str, int]], year: str) -> int:
    """Sum all 11 zones to get the NYCA total for a given year."""
    return sum(zone_data.get(year, {}).get(z, 0) for z in ZONES)


def _util_sum(zone_data: dict[str, dict[str, int]], year: str, zones: list[str]) -> int:
    return sum(zone_data.get(year, {}).get(z, 0) for z in zones)
```

```{python}
#| label: load-data

# Baseline tables
i3b_base = _parse_winter_str(PATH_BASELINE, "I-3b")   # Winter CP (total)
i3a_base = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_BASELINE, "I-3a")  # Summer CP (total)
i4b_base = _parse_winter_str(PATH_BASELINE, "I-4b")   # Winter NCP (non-coincident)
i13c_base = _parse_winter_str(PATH_BASELINE, "I-13c")  # Elec winter CP increment
i13b_base = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_BASELINE, "I-13b")  # Elec summer CP increment
i11d_base = _parse_winter_str(PATH_BASELINE, "I-11d")  # EV winter CP
i8c = _parse_winter_str(PATH_BASELINE, "I-8c")          # EE reductions (same both scenarios)
i10c = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_BASELINE, "I-10c")      # Non-solar DG reductions
i12c = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_BASELINE, "I-12c")      # Storage reductions
i14 = _parse_large_loads(PATH_BASELINE)                  # Large loads

# Lower scenario (only EV and electrification differ; shared reductions same as baseline)
i13c_low = _parse_winter_str(PATH_LOWER, "I-13c-L")    # Elec winter CP lower
i11d_low = _parse_winter_str(PATH_LOWER, "I-11d-L")    # EV winter CP lower
i3b_low = _parse_winter_str(PATH_LOWER, "I-3b-L")      # Winter CP lower (total)
i4b_low = _parse_winter_str(PATH_LOWER, "I-4b-L")      # Winter NCP lower
i14_low = _parse_large_loads(PATH_LOWER, sheet="I-14-L")  # Large loads lower

# Summer tables — CP totals and NCP (for Figures 5 and 6)
i3a_low  = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_LOWER, "I-3a-L")    # Summer CP lower (total)
i4a_base = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_BASELINE, "I-4a")   # Summer NCP baseline
i4a_low  = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_LOWER, "I-4a-L")    # Summer NCP lower

# Summer component tables (for Figure 6)
i13b_low  = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_LOWER, "I-13b-L")  # Elec summer CP lower
i11c_base = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_BASELINE, "I-11c") # EV summer CP
i11c_low  = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_LOWER, "I-11c-L")  # EV summer CP lower
i8b  = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_BASELINE, "I-8b")       # EE reductions summer
i10b = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_BASELINE, "I-10b")      # Non-solar DG summer
i12b = _parse_calendar_int(PATH_BASELINE, "I-12b")      # Storage summer
i14_summer     = _parse_large_loads_summer(PATH_BASELINE)              # Large loads summer
i14_summer_low = _parse_large_loads_summer(PATH_LOWER, sheet="I-14-L") # Large loads summer lower

# Headroom table from Synapse Energy Economics (Takahashi et al. 2024)
headroom = pl.read_csv(PATH_HEADROOM)

print("Headroom table:")
headroom
```

```{python}
#| label: forecast-years

all_years = sorted(
    yr for yr in i13c_base
    if FORECAST_START <= int(yr.split("-")[0]) <= FORECAST_END
)

print(f"Forecast years: {all_years[0]} → {all_years[-1]}  ({len(all_years)} years)")
```

## Figure 1: NYCA Building Electrification Incremental Winter Peak Load

Building electrification is the largest driver of winter peak growth in the NYISO
forecast. Table I-13c shows cumulative incremental additions to winter coincident
peak demand from building electrification by zone. We sum across all 11 zones to
get the NYCA-wide total.

The baseline scenario reflects NYISO's central case for heat pump adoption. The
lower demand scenario (I-13c-L) models slower adoption due to energy efficiency,
non-solar DG, storage, and lower EV uptake.

```{python}
#| label: fig-elec-load-growth
#| fig-cap: >
#|   NYCA building electrification cumulative winter coincident peak demand additions,
#|   2025–2055. Baseline scenario (blue) reflects NYISO's central case; lower scenario
#|   (orange) models slower adoption. Source: NYISO Gold Book 2025, Table I-13c.
#| fig-cap-location: margin

elec_rows = []
for yr_str in all_years:
    yr = int(yr_str.split("-")[0])
    elec_rows.append({
        "year": yr,
        "mw": _nyca(i13c_base, yr_str),
        "scenario": "Baseline",
    })
    elec_rows.append({
        "year": yr,
        "mw": _nyca(i13c_low, yr_str),
        "scenario": "Lower",
    })

df_elec = pl.DataFrame(elec_rows).sort(["scenario", "year"])

(
    ggplot(df_elec, aes("year", "mw", color="scenario"))
    + geom_line(size=1.3)
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=list(range(2025, 2056, 5)))
    + scale_y_continuous(labels=lambda lst: [f"{int(v):,}" for v in lst])
    + labs(
        title="NYCA Building Electrification Winter Coincident Peak Demand",
        subtitle=(
            "Cumulative incremental load additions by scenario\n"
            "NYISO Gold Book 2025, Table I-13c / I-13c-L"
        ),
        x="Winter season starting year",
        y="MW",
        color="Scenario",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(10.5, 5.0))
)
```

```{python}
#| label: elec-report-vars

last_yr = all_years[-1]
report_vars["elec_winter_mw_baseline_2055"] = _nyca(i13c_base, last_yr)
report_vars["elec_winter_mw_lower_2055"] = _nyca(i13c_low, last_yr)

print(
    f"By {last_yr}, building electrification adds:\n"
    f"  Baseline: {report_vars['elec_winter_mw_baseline_2055']:,} MW\n"
    f"  Lower:    {report_vars['elec_winter_mw_lower_2055']:,} MW"
)
```

## Figure 2: NYCA households converted to electric heating (image + logistic fit)

The NYISO Gold Book NYCA figure **Number of Residential Households Converted to
Electric Heating By Technology** is anchored to a **raster of that chart**
(`../img/nyiso_nyca_residential_electric_heating_by_technology_reference.jpg`, JPEG).
We record **cumulative band tops** (housing units) at each knot year on the graphic,
convert to **layer thicknesses**, and fit one **three-parameter logistic**
\(L/(1+e^{-k(t-t_0)})\) per layer to the **fraction of statewide residential
electric customers** at those knots (`scipy.optimize.curve_fit`, same structure as
rate-design-platform `fit_adoption_config.py`). **Ribbons** plot the fitted sigmoids
(stacked); **points** mark the digitized cumulative tops from the image (they need
not sit exactly on the smooth curves). Denominator: EIA-861 on S3.

```{python}
#| label: fig-nyca-electrified-housing-image
#| fig-cap: >
#|   NYCA residential households converted to electric heating by technology.
#|   Cumulative band edges digitized from the NYISO chart image; one logistic fit
#|   per layer to fraction of NY residential electric customers; stacked ribbons =
#|   fitted sigmoids. Points = digitized readings from the reference image.
#|   EIA-861: s3://data.sb/eia/861/electric_utility_stats/.
#| fig-cap-location: margin

S3_EIA861_BASE = "s3://data.sb/eia/861/electric_utility_stats/"
_AWS_S3_OPTS = {"aws_region": "us-west-2"}

_PATH_CHART_IMAGE = Path("../img/nyiso_nyca_residential_electric_heating_by_technology_reference.jpg")
if not _PATH_CHART_IMAGE.is_file():
    msg = f"Missing reference chart image: {_PATH_CHART_IMAGE.resolve()}"
    raise FileNotFoundError(msg)

# Load raster (JPEG) so the notebook is anchored to this file; re-digitize if replaced.
from matplotlib import image as _mpimg

_ref_img = _mpimg.imread(_PATH_CHART_IMAGE, format="jpeg")
if _ref_img.shape[:2] != (729, 1024):
    msg = (
        "Reference chart image dimensions changed; update cumulative tables above "
        "after re-digitizing the new raster."
    )
    raise ValueError(msg)

# Bottom → top stack order (matches legend / NYISO chart).
_TECH_ORDER: list[str] = [
    "ASHP full capacity",
    "ASHP dual fuel",
    "Electric resistance",
    "Ground source heat pump",
    "Supplemental heat",
]

_TECH_COLORS: dict[str, str] = {
    "ASHP full capacity": "#D55E00",
    "ASHP dual fuel": "#999999",
    "Electric resistance": "#F0E442",
    "Ground source heat pump": "#0072B2",
    "Supplemental heat": "#009E73",
}

# Cumulative stack tops (housing units) read from the reference image at each knot year.
# Row i = year _DIG_YEARS[i]; columns = upper bound of each band (same definitions as the chart).
_DIG_YEARS = np.array([2024, 2030, 2035, 2040, 2045, 2050, 2055, 2057], dtype=float)
_CUM_TOP_ASHP_FULL = np.array(
    [5_000, 100_000, 250_000, 580_000, 980_000, 1_400_000, 1_650_000, 1_750_000],
    dtype=float,
)
_CUM_TOP_DUAL = np.array(
    [7_000, 140_000, 350_000, 800_000, 1_380_000, 2_000_000, 2_350_000, 2_500_000],
    dtype=float,
)
_CUM_TOP_ER = np.array(
    [8_000, 160_000, 400_000, 900_000, 1_550_000, 2_250_000, 2_600_000, 2_750_000],
    dtype=float,
)
_CUM_TOP_GSHP = np.array(
    [9_000, 180_000, 450_000, 1_000_000, 1_720_000, 2_480_000, 2_850_000, 3_000_000],
    dtype=float,
)
_CUM_TOP_TOTAL = np.array(
    [10_000, 220_000, 550_000, 1_180_000, 2_050_000, 3_000_000, 3_450_000, 3_750_000],
    dtype=float,
)


def _latest_eia861_partition_year() -> int:
    import boto3

    client = boto3.client("s3", region_name=_AWS_S3_OPTS["aws_region"])
    prefix = "eia/861/electric_utility_stats/"
    years: list[int] = []
    paginator = client.get_paginator("list_objects_v2")
    for page in paginator.paginate(Bucket="data.sb", Prefix=prefix, Delimiter="/"):
        for cp in page.get("CommonPrefixes", []):
            name = cp["Prefix"].rstrip("/").split("/")[-1]
            if name.startswith("year="):
                years.append(int(name.split("=", 1)[1]))
    if not years:
        msg = f"No year= partitions under s3://data.sb/{prefix}"
        raise FileNotFoundError(msg)
    return max(years)


def load_ny_residential_electric_customers() -> tuple[float, int]:
    """Statewide sum of residential_customers from EIA-861 parquet on S3."""
    year = _latest_eia861_partition_year()
    path = f"{S3_EIA861_BASE}year={year}/state=NY/data.parquet"
    df = pl.read_parquet(path, storage_options=_AWS_S3_OPTS)
    total = float(df["residential_customers"].sum())
    return total, year


_eia_total, _eia_year = load_ny_residential_electric_customers()

_cum_series = [
    _CUM_TOP_ASHP_FULL,
    _CUM_TOP_DUAL,
    _CUM_TOP_ER,
    _CUM_TOP_GSHP,
    _CUM_TOP_TOTAL,
]
_cum_mat = np.stack(_cum_series, axis=0)  # (5, n_knot): cumulative tops per band
_layer_bottoms = np.vstack([np.zeros((1, _cum_mat.shape[1])), _cum_mat[:-1]])
_layer_thickness_hu = _cum_mat - _layer_bottoms  # housing units per layer at each knot


def _logistic(t: np.ndarray, L: float, k: float, t0: float) -> np.ndarray:
    return L / (1.0 + np.exp(-k * (t - t0)))


def _fit_logistic_layer(years: np.ndarray, fracs: np.ndarray) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
    """Fit logistic to (years, fracs); bounds match `fit_adoption_config._fit_logistic`."""
    L_min = float(fracs.max()) * 1.01
    p0 = [float(fracs.max()) * 1.5, 0.10, 2045.0]
    bounds = ([L_min, 0.001, 2020.0], [1.0, 1.0, 2080.0])
    popt, _ = curve_fit(
        _logistic, years, fracs, p0=p0, bounds=bounds, maxfev=50_000
    )
    return float(popt[0]), float(popt[1]), float(popt[2])


_fit_params: list[tuple[float, float, float]] = []
for j, lab in enumerate(_TECH_ORDER):
    fr = _layer_thickness_hu[j] / _eia_total
    par = _fit_logistic_layer(_DIG_YEARS, fr)
    _fit_params.append(par)
    pred = _logistic(_DIG_YEARS, *par)
    rmse = float(np.sqrt(np.mean((pred - fr) ** 2)))
    print(f"{lab}: L,k,t0 = {par}  RMSE(frac)={rmse:.5f}")

_t_fine = np.linspace(2024.0, 2057.0, 400)
_layer_pct: list[np.ndarray] = [
    100.0 * _logistic(_t_fine, *_fit_params[j]) for j in range(len(_TECH_ORDER))
]

_stack_rows: list[dict] = []
for i, yr in enumerate(_t_fine):
    cum_pct = 0.0
    for j, lab in enumerate(_TECH_ORDER):
        top = _layer_pct[j][i]
        lo, hi = cum_pct, cum_pct + top
        _stack_rows.append({
            "year": yr,
            "technology": lab,
            "ymin": lo,
            "ymax": hi,
        })
        cum_pct = hi

_df_stack = pl.DataFrame(_stack_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("technology").cast(pl.Enum(_TECH_ORDER)),
)

_pt_rows: list[dict] = []
for i, y in enumerate(_DIG_YEARS):
    for j, lab in enumerate(_TECH_ORDER):
        hi_hu = float(_cum_mat[j, i])
        _pt_rows.append({
            "year": y,
            "technology": lab,
            "y_top": 100.0 * hi_hu / _eia_total,
        })

_df_pts = pl.DataFrame(_pt_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("technology").cast(pl.Enum(_TECH_ORDER)),
)

print(
    f"Reference image: {_PATH_CHART_IMAGE.resolve()}\n"
    f"EIA-861 NY residential electric customers: {_eia_total:,.0f} ({_eia_year} data year)"
)

report_vars["eia_ny_residential_customers"] = _eia_total
report_vars["eia_ny_residential_data_year"] = float(_eia_year)
report_vars["nyca_electrified_pct_2057_image_stack"] = float(
    100.0 * _CUM_TOP_TOTAL[-1] / _eia_total
)
report_vars["nyca_electrified_pct_2057_logistic_stack"] = float(
    sum(
        _logistic(np.array([2057.0]), *_fit_params[j])[0] * 100.0
        for j in range(len(_TECH_ORDER))
    )
)

(
    ggplot()
    + geom_ribbon(
        _df_stack,
        aes(x="year", ymin="ymin", ymax="ymax", fill="technology"),
        alpha=0.88,
    )
    + geom_point(
        _df_pts,
        aes(x="year", y="y_top", fill="technology"),
        color="#333333",
        size=2.2,
        stroke=0.25,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=_TECH_COLORS, name="Technology")
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=list(range(2024, 2058, 2)))
    + scale_y_continuous(
        labels=lambda xs: [f"{v:.0f}%" for v in xs],
        expand=(0, 0, 0.05, 0),
    )
    + labs(
        title="Number of residential households converted to electric heating by technology — NYCA",
        subtitle=(
            "Digitized cumulative tops from reference image; one logistic (sigmoid) fit per layer; "
            f"y-axis = % of {_eia_total:,.0f} NY residential electric customers (EIA-861 {_eia_year})."
        ),
        x="Year",
        y="% of NY residential electric customers (EIA-861)",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(11.0, 5.5), legend_position="bottom")
)
```

## Figure 3: NYCA Coincident Peak Demand — Summer and Winter Forecast

This figure mirrors the structure of Figure 17 from the RI Peak Report. The summer
peak (orange) consistently exceeds the winter peak (blue) — but building
electrification is dramatically narrowing that gap.

Solid lines show the baseline forecast including all demand growth. Dashed lines
show the counterfactual without building electrification (I-3b minus I-13c for
winter; I-3a minus I-13b for summer). The shaded ribbon between them shows the
electrification contribution.

```{python}
#| label: fig-peak-forecast
#| fig-cap: >
#|   NYCA coincident peak demand forecast, 2025–2055. Solid lines include building
#|   electrification; dashed lines show the counterfactual without it. Shaded ribbons
#|   show the electrification contribution. Building electrification nearly closes
#|   the gap between summer and winter peaks by mid-century. Source: NYISO Gold Book
#|   2025, Tables I-3a, I-3b, I-13b, I-13c.
#| fig-cap-location: margin
#TODO-start at 0 add labels for colors
peak_rows = []
for yr_str in all_years:
    yr = int(yr_str.split("-")[0])
    winter_with = _nyca(i3b_base, yr_str)
    summer_with = _nyca(i3a_base, yr_str)
    winter_without = winter_with - _nyca(i13c_base, yr_str)
    summer_without = summer_with - _nyca(i13b_base, yr_str)
    peak_rows.append({
        "year": yr,
        "winter_with": winter_with,
        "winter_without": winter_without,
        "summer_with": summer_with,
        "summer_without": summer_without,
    })

df_peak = pl.DataFrame(peak_rows)

# Initial winter-to-summer ratio annotation
first = df_peak.row(0, named=True)
ratio_pct = f"{first['winter_with'] / first['summer_with']:.0%}"
mid_y = (first["winter_with"] + first["summer_with"]) / 2

# Y-axis: zoom to data range. Bottom is rounded down to nearest 5,000 MW below
# the minimum without-electrification value; top is padded 5% above the maximum.
_y_min = min(df_peak["winter_without"].min(), df_peak["summer_without"].min())
_y_max = max(df_peak["winter_with"].max(), df_peak["summer_with"].max())
_y_lo = max(0, (_y_min // 5000) * 5000)
_y_hi = _y_max * 1.05

(
    ggplot()
    # Winter ribbon (electrification contribution)
    + geom_ribbon(
        df_peak,
        aes(x="year", ymin="winter_without", ymax="winter_with"),
        fill=SB_COLORS["sky"],
        alpha=0.25,
    )
    # Summer ribbon
    + geom_ribbon(
        df_peak,
        aes(x="year", ymin="summer_without", ymax="summer_with"),
        fill=SB_COLORS["carrot"],
        alpha=0.25,
    )
    # Solid lines — with electrification
    + geom_line(
        df_peak, aes(x="year", y="winter_with"),
        color=SB_COLORS["sky"], size=1.3,
    )
    + geom_line(
        df_peak, aes(x="year", y="summer_with"),
        color=SB_COLORS["carrot"], size=1.3,
    )
    # Dashed lines — without electrification
    + geom_line(
        df_peak, aes(x="year", y="winter_without"),
        color=SB_COLORS["sky"], size=1.0, linetype="dashed",
    )
    + geom_line(
        df_peak, aes(x="year", y="summer_without"),
        color=SB_COLORS["carrot"], size=1.0, linetype="dashed",
    )
    # Bracket and ratio annotation
    + annotate(
        "segment",
        x=2025, xend=2025,
        y=first["winter_with"], yend=first["summer_with"],
        color="#555555", size=0.7,
    )
    + annotate(
        "text",
        x=2025.6, y=mid_y,
        label=ratio_pct,
        size=11, ha="left", color="#333333",
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=list(range(2025, 2056, 5)))
    + scale_y_continuous(labels=lambda lst: [f"{int(v):,}" for v in lst])
    + coord_cartesian(ylim=(_y_lo, _y_hi))
    + labs(
        title="NYCA Coincident Peak Demand Forecast — Baseline Scenario",
        subtitle=(
            "Solid = with building electrification; dashed = without; ribbon = electrification contribution\n"
            "Source: NYISO Gold Book 2025, Tables I-3a, I-3b, I-13b, I-13c"
        ),
        x="Year",
        y="Net peak (MW)",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(10.5, 5.5))
)
```

```{python}
#| label: peak-report-vars

last_yr = all_years[-1]
report_vars["winter_peak_baseline_2055"] = _nyca(i3b_base, last_yr)
report_vars["summer_peak_baseline_2055"] = _nyca(i3a_base, last_yr)
report_vars["elec_contribution_winter_2055"] = _nyca(i13c_base, last_yr)
report_vars["winter_summer_ratio_2025"] = (
    _nyca(i3b_base, all_years[0]) / _nyca(i3a_base, all_years[0])
)

print(
    f"By {last_yr}:\n"
    f"  Winter peak (baseline):           {report_vars['winter_peak_baseline_2055']:,} MW\n"
    f"  Summer peak (baseline):           {report_vars['summer_peak_baseline_2055']:,} MW\n"
    f"  Electrification contribution:     {report_vars['elec_contribution_winter_2055']:,} MW\n"
    f"  Initial winter/summer ratio:      {report_vars['winter_summer_ratio_2025']:.0%}"
)
```

## Figure 4: Per-Utility Demand Components

This replicates the demand decomposition from the rate-design-platform's
`compute_headroom_exhaustion_year.py`, rebuilt in the Switchbox plotnine style,
now showing **both the baseline and lower demand scenarios** side by side.

Each panel pair shows one investor-owned distribution utility's winter coincident
peak demand components as a percentage of its 2023-24 coincident winter peak.
Additions (building electrification, EV load, large loads) stack above zero;
reductions (energy efficiency, non-solar DG, BTM storage) stack below.

A **solid black line** shows total CP growth vs. 2023-24 (I-3b). A **dashed red
vertical line** marks the year NCP (I-4b) exceeds the Synapse headroom threshold,
with the exhaustion year labeled. Utilities where headroom is not exhausted within
the forecast window show a "Not exhausted" label in the top-right corner.

**Headroom logic.** Exhaustion is the first season (from 2024-25 onward in each
scenario’s I-4b table) where **absolute** winter **non-coincident** peak (I-4b zone
sum) is at least **(1 + Synapse headroom %) ×** the same zone-sum at
`HEADROOM_BASELINE_YEAR` (**2023-24**), always read from the **baseline** workbook so
the anchor exists even when lower-demand I-4b-L omits historical rows. The stacked
ribbons use **coincident** peak (I-3b) normalized to `NORM_YEAR` (**2023-24**);
headroom uses the **same winter season** for the I-4b NCP anchor (`HEADROOM_BASELINE_YEAR`).

**Gold Book vintage.** This notebook uses the **2025** Gold Book from S3; RDP’s
defaults used **2024** files, so forecast levels can differ.

**Zone lists** match `compute_headroom_exhaustion_year.py`: National Grid and NYSEG &
RGE both sum **A–F**; Con Edison sums **G, H, I, J**; Central Hudson and Orange &
Rockland each sum **G** only. Zone **K** (LIPA) is excluded.

```{python}
#| label: build-component-data

COMPONENT_COLORS: dict[str, str] = {
    "Building electrification": SB_COLORS["sky"],
    "EV load": SB_COLORS["saffron"],
    "Large loads": SB_COLORS["carrot"],
    "EE & codes/standards": SB_COLORS["pistachio"],
    "Non-solar DG": "#7b6ea0",
    "BTM storage": "#b0722a",
}


# Per-scenario CP tables and component tables
SCENARIOS: dict[str, dict] = {
    "Baseline": {
        "i3b": i3b_base,
        "i4b": i4b_base,
        "pos": [
            ("Building electrification", i13c_base),
            ("EV load", i11d_base),
            ("Large loads", i14),
        ],
        "neg": [
            ("EE & codes/standards", i8c),
            ("Non-solar DG", i10c),
            ("BTM storage", i12c),
        ],
    },
    "Lower demand": {
        "i3b": i3b_low,
        "i4b": i4b_low,
        "pos": [
            ("Building electrification", i13c_low),
            ("EV load", i11d_low),
            ("Large loads", i14_low),
        ],
        "neg": [
            # EE, DG, storage not separately published for lower scenario;
            # reuse baseline (conservative — lower scenario reductions likely larger)
            ("EE & codes/standards", i8c),
            ("Non-solar DG", i10c),
            ("BTM storage", i12c),
        ],
    },
}

component_order = (
    [c for c, _ in SCENARIOS["Baseline"]["pos"]]
    + [c for c, _ in SCENARIOS["Baseline"]["neg"]]
)

# Build stacked ribbon data and CP growth lines for both scenarios
stack_rows = []
growth_rows = []
headroom_rows = []

for scenario, sc in SCENARIOS.items():
    # Same horizon logic as RDP: scan I-4b rows from 2024-25 onward (keys present in
    # this scenario’s file — lower-demand I-4b-L may omit 2024-25).
    headroom_years = sorted(
        yr for yr in sc["i4b"]
        if int(yr.split("-")[0]) >= 2024 and int(yr.split("-")[0]) <= FORECAST_END
    )

    for utility, zones in UTILITY_ZONES.items():
        base_cp = _util_sum(i3b_base, NORM_YEAR, zones)
        if base_cp == 0:
            continue

        # Absolute NCP level vs threshold (I-4b zone sum, same zones as RDP).
        ncp_base = _util_sum(i4b_base, HEADROOM_BASELINE_YEAR, zones)
        threshold = ncp_base * (1 + HEADROOM_PCT[utility])
        ex_yr: int | None = None
        for yr_str in headroom_years:
            if _util_sum(sc["i4b"], yr_str, zones) >= threshold:
                ex_yr = int(yr_str.split("-")[0])
                break

        label = (
            f"Headroom\nexhausted\n{ex_yr}–{ex_yr - 1999:02d}"
            if ex_yr is not None
            else "Not exhausted\nwithin forecast"
        )

        headroom_rows.append({
            "scenario": scenario,
            "utility": utility,
            "ex_year": ex_yr if ex_yr is not None else -1,
            "exhausted": ex_yr is not None,
            "label": label,
        })

        for yr_str in all_years:
            yr = int(yr_str.split("-")[0])

            pos_bottom = 0.0
            for comp_label, data in sc["pos"]:
                raw = _util_sum(data, yr_str, zones)
                pct = 100.0 * raw / base_cp
                stack_rows.append({
                    "scenario": scenario, "utility": utility, "year": yr,
                    "component": comp_label, "ymin": pos_bottom, "ymax": pos_bottom + pct,
                })
                pos_bottom += pct
            neg_bottom = 0.0
            for comp_label, data in sc["neg"]:
                raw = _util_sum(data, yr_str, zones)
                pct = 100.0 * raw / base_cp
                stack_rows.append({
                    "scenario": scenario, "utility": utility, "year": yr,
                    "component": comp_label, "ymin": neg_bottom - pct, "ymax": neg_bottom,
                })
                neg_bottom -= pct

            cp_val = _util_sum(sc["i3b"], yr_str, zones)
            growth_rows.append({
                "scenario": scenario, "utility": utility, "year": yr,
                "cp_growth_pct": 100.0 * (cp_val - base_cp) / base_cp,
            })

df_stack = pl.DataFrame(stack_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(component_order)),
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(["Baseline", "Lower demand"])),
)
df_growth = pl.DataFrame(growth_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(["Baseline", "Lower demand"])),
)
df_headroom = pl.DataFrame(headroom_rows)

# Facet label: "Utility — Scenario"
df_stack = df_stack.with_columns(
    (pl.col("utility") + " — " + pl.col("scenario")).alias("facet")
)
df_growth = df_growth.with_columns(
    (pl.col("utility") + " — " + pl.col("scenario")).alias("facet")
)
df_headroom = df_headroom.with_columns(
    (pl.col("utility") + " — " + pl.col("scenario")).alias("facet")
)

# Natural facet order: utility order × scenario order
facet_order = [
    f"{u} — {s}"
    for u in UTILITY_ZONES
    for s in ["Baseline", "Lower demand"]
]
df_stack = df_stack.with_columns(pl.col("facet").cast(pl.Enum(facet_order)))
df_growth = df_growth.with_columns(pl.col("facet").cast(pl.Enum(facet_order)))
df_headroom = df_headroom.with_columns(pl.col("facet").cast(pl.Enum(facet_order)))

print("Headroom exhaustion summary:")
print(df_headroom.select(["facet", "ex_year", "exhausted"]))
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-demand-components
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Winter coincident peak demand components by utility, baseline vs. lower demand
#|   scenario, as % of each utility's 2023-24 coincident winter peak (I-3b). Stacked
#|   ribbons show additions (building electrification, EV, large loads) above zero and
#|   reductions (EE, non-solar DG, storage) below. Solid black line = total CP growth
#|   vs. 2023-24. Red dashed line = first year absolute winter NCP (I-4b, RDP zone
#|   lists) reaches (1 + Synapse %) × 2023-24 NCP from the baseline workbook.
#|   NYISO Gold Book 2025; Synapse Energy Economics July 2024.
#| fig-cap-location: margin
#| fig-height: 34
#| fig-width: 24

# Plotnine refuses saves > 25 in by default; this figure is 24×34 in.
from plotnine.options import set_option

set_option("limitsize", False)

# Vlines for exhausted utilities
df_vline = df_headroom.filter(pl.col("exhausted"))

# For the "not exhausted" text label, we need a y position near the top of each
# panel. Use a fixed high percentage since panels have free_y scales.
# We'll annotate using geom_text at x=2055 (right edge), placed per-facet.
df_not_exhausted = df_headroom.filter(~pl.col("exhausted"))

# y position for exhaustion label: place just below the max ymax of the ribbon
# for each facet. Compute per-facet max of positive stack top.
_pos_tops = (
    df_stack.filter(pl.col("ymax") > 0)
    .group_by("facet")
    .agg(pl.col("ymax").max().alias("top"))
)
df_vline = df_vline.join(_pos_tops, on="facet", how="left")
df_not_exhausted = df_not_exhausted.join(_pos_tops, on="facet", how="left")

(
    ggplot()
    + geom_ribbon(
        df_stack,
        aes(x="year", ymin="ymin", ymax="ymax", fill="component"),
        alpha=0.85,
    )
    + geom_hline(yintercept=0, color="black", size=0.6)
    + geom_line(
        df_growth,
        aes(x="year", y="cp_growth_pct"),
        color="black", size=1.2, linetype="solid",
    )
    + geom_vline(
        df_vline,
        aes(xintercept="ex_year"),
        color="#cc2222", size=1.0, linetype="dashed",
    )
    + geom_text(
        df_vline,
        aes(x="ex_year", y="top", label="label"),
        color="#cc2222", size=8, ha="left", va="top", nudge_x=0.4,
    )
    + geom_text(
        df_not_exhausted,
        aes(x=2055, y="top", label="label"),
        color="#cc2222", size=8, ha="right", va="top",
    )
    + facet_wrap("facet", ncol=2, scales="free_y")
    + scale_fill_manual(
        values=COMPONENT_COLORS,
        name="Component",
        breaks=component_order,
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=list(range(2025, 2056, 5)))
    + scale_y_continuous(
        labels=lambda lst: [f"{v:.0f}%" for v in lst],
    )
    + labs(
        title="Winter Coincident Peak Demand Components by Utility — Baseline vs. Lower Demand",
        subtitle=(
            "As % of 2023-24 coincident winter peak (I-3b) — NYISO Gold Book 2025\n"
            "Black line = total CP growth; red dashed = headroom exhaustion (Synapse 2024)"
        ),
        x="Winter season starting year",
        y="% of 2023–24 peak",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(24, 34))
)
```

## Figure 5: Per-Utility Winter vs. Summer Coincident Peak Growth

The previous figure decomposed winter peak growth into its components. Here we step
back and compare **total** coincident peak growth for winter (I-3b) and summer (I-3a)
side by side, using the same per-utility facet layout. This reveals how quickly
winter peak growth is closing the gap with — or overtaking — summer.

```{python}
#| label: build-season-growth-data

season_growth_rows: list[dict] = []

SEASON_TABLES: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = {
    "Baseline": {"winter": i3b_base, "summer": i3a_base},
    "Lower demand": {"winter": i3b_low, "summer": i3a_low},
}

for scenario, tables in SEASON_TABLES.items():
    for utility, zones in UTILITY_ZONES.items():
        base_cp = _util_sum(i3a_base, NORM_YEAR, zones)
        if base_cp == 0:
            continue

        for yr_str in all_years:
            yr = int(yr_str.split("-")[0])
            for season_name, cp_table in tables.items():
                cp_val = _util_sum(cp_table, yr_str, zones)
                season_growth_rows.append({
                    "scenario": scenario,
                    "utility": utility,
                    "year": yr,
                    "season": season_name.capitalize(),
                    "cp_growth_pct": 100.0 * (cp_val - base_cp) / base_cp,
                })

df_season_growth = pl.DataFrame(season_growth_rows).with_columns(
    pl.col("season").cast(pl.Enum(["Winter", "Summer"])),
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(["Baseline", "Lower demand"])),
)
df_season_growth = df_season_growth.with_columns(
    (pl.col("utility") + " — " + pl.col("scenario")).alias("facet")
)
df_season_growth = df_season_growth.with_columns(
    pl.col("facet").cast(pl.Enum(facet_order))
)

# Find the crossover year where winter CP first exceeds summer CP (in absolute MW).
# Both are normalized to the same summer base, so winter > summer iff
# winter_growth_pct > summer_growth_pct.
crossover_rows: list[dict] = []
for scenario, tables in SEASON_TABLES.items():
    for utility, zones in UTILITY_ZONES.items():
        facet_label = f"{utility} — {scenario}"
        prev_diff: float | None = None
        prev_yr: int | None = None
        for yr_str in all_years:
            yr = int(yr_str.split("-")[0])
            w_val = _util_sum(tables["winter"], yr_str, zones)
            s_val = _util_sum(tables["summer"], yr_str, zones)
            diff = float(w_val - s_val)
            if diff >= 0:
                # Interpolate between previous year (winter < summer) and this year
                if prev_diff is not None and prev_yr is not None and prev_diff < 0:
                    frac = -prev_diff / (diff - prev_diff)
                    cross_x = prev_yr + frac
                else:
                    cross_x = float(yr)
                cross_yr_int = round(cross_x)
                crossover_rows.append({
                    "facet": facet_label,
                    "cross_year": cross_x,
                    "label": f"Winter ≥ summer\n~{cross_yr_int}–{cross_yr_int - 1999:02d}",
                })
                break
            prev_diff = diff
            prev_yr = yr

df_crossover = pl.DataFrame(crossover_rows)
if not df_crossover.is_empty():
    df_crossover = df_crossover.with_columns(
        pl.col("facet").cast(pl.Enum(facet_order))
    )

print("Season growth data shape:", df_season_growth.shape)
print(df_season_growth.head(6))
print("\nCrossover years:")
print(df_crossover)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-season-growth
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Winter vs. summer coincident peak demand growth by utility and scenario,
#|   both as % of the 2023-24 summer coincident peak (I-3a). Winter (blue)
#|   starts below zero (since winter < summer in 2023-24) and grows rapidly —
#|   building electrification is a winter-peaking phenomenon. Green dashed line
#|   marks the year winter CP first exceeds summer CP. Red dashed line marks
#|   headroom exhaustion (Synapse 2024). NYISO Gold Book 2025, Tables I-3a, I-3b.
#| fig-cap-location: margin
#| fig-height: 17
#| fig-width: 24

from plotnine.options import set_option

set_option("limitsize", False)

# Reuse headroom data from Figure 4 (winter NCP-based)
_df_hr_vline = df_headroom.filter(pl.col("exhausted"))
_df_hr_no = df_headroom.filter(~pl.col("exhausted"))

# y positions for labels: use per-facet max of the growth lines
_season_tops = (
    df_season_growth
    .group_by("facet")
    .agg(pl.col("cp_growth_pct").max().alias("top"))
)
_df_hr_vline = _df_hr_vline.join(_season_tops, on="facet", how="left")
_df_hr_no = _df_hr_no.join(_season_tops, on="facet", how="left")

_df_cross_labeled = df_crossover.join(_season_tops, on="facet", how="left") if not df_crossover.is_empty() else df_crossover

(
    ggplot(df_season_growth, aes(x="year", y="cp_growth_pct", color="season"))
    + geom_hline(yintercept=0, color="black", size=0.5)
    + geom_line(size=1.2)
    # Headroom exhaustion vlines (from Figure 4, winter NCP)
    + geom_vline(
        _df_hr_vline,
        aes(xintercept="ex_year"),
        color="#cc2222", size=1.0, linetype="dashed",
    )
    + geom_text(
        _df_hr_vline,
        aes(x="ex_year", y="top", label="label"),
        color="#cc2222", size=8, ha="left", va="top", nudge_x=0.4,
    )
    + geom_text(
        _df_hr_no,
        aes(x=2055, y="top", label="label"),
        color="#cc2222", size=8, ha="right", va="top",
    )
    # Crossover vlines (winter ≥ summer)
    + (geom_vline(
        _df_cross_labeled,
        aes(xintercept="cross_year"),
        color=SB_COLORS["pistachio"], size=1.0, linetype="dashed",
    ) if not df_crossover.is_empty() else geom_hline(yintercept=-9999, alpha=0))
    + (geom_text(
        _df_cross_labeled,
        aes(x="cross_year", y="top", label="label"),
        color=SB_COLORS["pistachio"], size=8, ha="right", va="top", nudge_x=-0.4,
    ) if not df_crossover.is_empty() else geom_hline(yintercept=-9999, alpha=0))
    + facet_wrap("facet", ncol=2, scales="free_y")
    + scale_color_manual(
        values={"Winter": SB_COLORS["sky"], "Summer": SB_COLORS["carrot"]},
        name="Season",
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=list(range(2025, 2056, 5)))
    + scale_y_continuous(
        labels=lambda lst: [f"{v:.0f}%" for v in lst],
    )
    + labs(
        title="Winter vs. Summer Coincident Peak Growth by Utility — Baseline vs. Lower Demand",
        subtitle=(
            "Both seasons as % of 2023-24 summer coincident peak (I-3a) — NYISO Gold Book 2025\n"
            "Red dashed = headroom exhaustion (Synapse 2024); green dashed = winter ≥ summer crossover"
        ),
        x="Year",
        y="% of 2023–24 summer peak",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(24, 17))
)
```

## Figure 6: Per-Utility Summer Demand Components

This mirrors the winter demand component decomposition (Figure 4), but for **summer**
coincident peak demand (I-3a). The same six components are shown using summer-specific
Gold Book tables: building electrification (I-13b), EV load (I-11c), large loads
(I-14 summer section), energy efficiency (I-8b), non-solar DG (I-10b), and BTM
storage (I-12b).

Headroom exhaustion uses the same Synapse headroom percentages as winter — no
summer-specific thresholds are available, so the winter thresholds serve as a
conservative proxy.

```{python}
#| label: build-component-data-summer

SCENARIOS_SUMMER: dict[str, dict] = {
    "Baseline": {
        "i3a": i3a_base,
        "i4a": i4a_base,
        "pos": [
            ("Building electrification", i13b_base),
            ("EV load", i11c_base),
            ("Large loads", i14_summer),
        ],
        "neg": [
            ("EE & codes/standards", i8b),
            ("Non-solar DG", i10b),
            ("BTM storage", i12b),
        ],
    },
    "Lower demand": {
        "i3a": i3a_low,
        "i4a": i4a_low,
        "pos": [
            ("Building electrification", i13b_low),
            ("EV load", i11c_low),
            ("Large loads", i14_summer_low),
        ],
        "neg": [
            ("EE & codes/standards", i8b),
            ("Non-solar DG", i10b),
            ("BTM storage", i12b),
        ],
    },
}

stack_rows_s: list[dict] = []
growth_rows_s: list[dict] = []
headroom_rows_s: list[dict] = []

for scenario, sc in SCENARIOS_SUMMER.items():
    headroom_years_s = sorted(
        yr for yr in sc["i4a"]
        if int(yr.split("-")[0]) >= 2024 and int(yr.split("-")[0]) <= FORECAST_END
    )

    for utility, zones in UTILITY_ZONES.items():
        base_cp_s = _util_sum(i3a_base, NORM_YEAR, zones)
        if base_cp_s == 0:
            continue

        # Summer headroom: NCP (I-4a) vs threshold (winter Synapse % as proxy)
        ncp_base_s = _util_sum(i4a_base, HEADROOM_BASELINE_YEAR, zones)
        threshold_s = ncp_base_s * (1 + HEADROOM_PCT[utility])
        ex_yr_s: int | None = None
        for yr_str in headroom_years_s:
            if _util_sum(sc["i4a"], yr_str, zones) >= threshold_s:
                ex_yr_s = int(yr_str.split("-")[0])
                break

        label_s = (
            f"Headroom\nexhausted\n{ex_yr_s}–{ex_yr_s - 1999:02d}"
            if ex_yr_s is not None
            else "Not exhausted\nwithin forecast"
        )

        headroom_rows_s.append({
            "scenario": scenario,
            "utility": utility,
            "ex_year": ex_yr_s if ex_yr_s is not None else -1,
            "exhausted": ex_yr_s is not None,
            "label": label_s,
        })

        for yr_str in all_years:
            yr = int(yr_str.split("-")[0])

            pos_bottom = 0.0
            for comp_label, data in sc["pos"]:
                raw = _util_sum(data, yr_str, zones)
                pct = 100.0 * raw / base_cp_s
                stack_rows_s.append({
                    "scenario": scenario, "utility": utility, "year": yr,
                    "component": comp_label, "ymin": pos_bottom, "ymax": pos_bottom + pct,
                })
                pos_bottom += pct
            neg_bottom = 0.0
            for comp_label, data in sc["neg"]:
                raw = _util_sum(data, yr_str, zones)
                pct = 100.0 * raw / base_cp_s
                stack_rows_s.append({
                    "scenario": scenario, "utility": utility, "year": yr,
                    "component": comp_label, "ymin": neg_bottom - pct, "ymax": neg_bottom,
                })
                neg_bottom -= pct

            cp_val_s = _util_sum(sc["i3a"], yr_str, zones)
            growth_rows_s.append({
                "scenario": scenario, "utility": utility, "year": yr,
                "cp_growth_pct": 100.0 * (cp_val_s - base_cp_s) / base_cp_s,
            })

df_stack_s = pl.DataFrame(stack_rows_s).with_columns(
    pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(component_order)),
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(["Baseline", "Lower demand"])),
)
df_growth_s = pl.DataFrame(growth_rows_s).with_columns(
    pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(["Baseline", "Lower demand"])),
)
df_headroom_s = pl.DataFrame(headroom_rows_s)

df_stack_s = df_stack_s.with_columns(
    (pl.col("utility") + " — " + pl.col("scenario")).alias("facet")
)
df_growth_s = df_growth_s.with_columns(
    (pl.col("utility") + " — " + pl.col("scenario")).alias("facet")
)
df_headroom_s = df_headroom_s.with_columns(
    (pl.col("utility") + " — " + pl.col("scenario")).alias("facet")
)
df_stack_s = df_stack_s.with_columns(pl.col("facet").cast(pl.Enum(facet_order)))
df_growth_s = df_growth_s.with_columns(pl.col("facet").cast(pl.Enum(facet_order)))
df_headroom_s = df_headroom_s.with_columns(pl.col("facet").cast(pl.Enum(facet_order)))

print("Summer headroom exhaustion summary:")
print(df_headroom_s.select(["facet", "ex_year", "exhausted"]))
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-demand-components-summer
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Summer coincident peak demand components by utility, baseline vs. lower demand
#|   scenario, as % of each utility's 2023-24 coincident summer peak (I-3a). Stacked
#|   ribbons show additions (building electrification, EV, large loads) above zero and
#|   reductions (EE, non-solar DG, storage) below. Solid black line = total summer CP
#|   growth vs. 2023-24. Red dashed line = first year absolute summer NCP (I-4a)
#|   reaches (1 + Synapse %) × 2023-24 NCP (winter headroom thresholds used as proxy).
#|   NYISO Gold Book 2025; Synapse Energy Economics July 2024.
#| fig-cap-location: margin
#| fig-height: 34
#| fig-width: 24

from plotnine.options import set_option

set_option("limitsize", False)

df_vline_s = df_headroom_s.filter(pl.col("exhausted"))
df_not_exhausted_s = df_headroom_s.filter(~pl.col("exhausted"))

_pos_tops_s = (
    df_stack_s.filter(pl.col("ymax") > 0)
    .group_by("facet")
    .agg(pl.col("ymax").max().alias("top"))
)
df_vline_s = df_vline_s.join(_pos_tops_s, on="facet", how="left")
df_not_exhausted_s = df_not_exhausted_s.join(_pos_tops_s, on="facet", how="left")

(
    ggplot()
    + geom_ribbon(
        df_stack_s,
        aes(x="year", ymin="ymin", ymax="ymax", fill="component"),
        alpha=0.85,
    )
    + geom_hline(yintercept=0, color="black", size=0.6)
    + geom_line(
        df_growth_s,
        aes(x="year", y="cp_growth_pct"),
        color="black", size=1.2, linetype="solid",
    )
    + geom_vline(
        df_vline_s,
        aes(xintercept="ex_year"),
        color="#cc2222", size=1.0, linetype="dashed",
    )
    + geom_text(
        df_vline_s,
        aes(x="ex_year", y="top", label="label"),
        color="#cc2222", size=8, ha="left", va="top", nudge_x=0.4,
    )
    + geom_text(
        df_not_exhausted_s,
        aes(x=2055, y="top", label="label"),
        color="#cc2222", size=8, ha="right", va="top",
    )
    + facet_wrap("facet", ncol=2, scales="free_y")
    + scale_fill_manual(
        values=COMPONENT_COLORS,
        name="Component",
        breaks=component_order,
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=list(range(2025, 2056, 5)))
    + scale_y_continuous(
        labels=lambda lst: [f"{v:.0f}%" for v in lst],
    )
    + labs(
        title="Summer Coincident Peak Demand Components by Utility — Baseline vs. Lower Demand",
        subtitle=(
            "As % of 2023-24 coincident summer peak (I-3a) — NYISO Gold Book 2025\n"
            "Black line = total summer CP growth; red dashed = headroom exhaustion (winter proxy, Synapse 2024)"
        ),
        x="Year",
        y="% of 2023–24 summer peak",
    )
    + theme_switchbox()
    + theme(figure_size=(24, 34))
)
```

## Report variables

```{python}
#| label: export-report-vars

from plotnine.options import set_option

set_option("limitsize", True)

Path("../cache/gold_book_report_vars.pkl").write_bytes(pickle.dumps(report_vars))
print("Exported report vars:")
for k, v in report_vars.items():
    print(f"  {k}: {v:,.0f}" if isinstance(v, float) and v > 1 else f"  {k}: {v:.1%}" if isinstance(v, float) else f"  {k}: {v:,}")
```
